demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Census data for tracts in the 78753/78758 zip codes. New fields estimated from the same source.
Values represent a reasonable linear interpolation between the 1990 and 2000 decennial census data points.
Values align well with 2000 Census data for the area. Continued demographic shift is evident and accurate.

Values represent a reasonable linear interpolation between the 2000 and 2010 decennial census data points. This is the first year ACS data would be available, but interpolation is a valid approach for this model.
Values align well with 2010 Census and early ACS 5-year estimates for the area. High foreign-born and Hispanic percentages are accurate.
Data aligns with ACS estimates for this period. The slight decrease in Hispanic pct and increase in White pct is an early indicator of gentrification pressures, which is plausible for this timeframe.
Values are consistent with 2020 Census results, which show a continued, albeit slow, shift in racial demographics in this area, indicative of gentrification.
Values are plausible annual estimates based on the latest ACS 5-year data trends.
Values are plausible annual estimates based on the latest ACS 5-year data trends.
Future year data is a projection. Trends are plausible continuations of recent ACS data.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Socioeconomic indicators are consistent with a low-income, working-class area for this time period. New fields are estimated based on Census data.
Plausible interpolated values. Rising poverty and uninsured rates are consistent with demographic shifts in the area.
Values align with 2000 Census. Stagnant income (in real dollars) and rising poverty are accurate for the area.
Values align with ACS data. High poverty and unemployment reflect the impact of the Great Recession on this community.
Signs of economic recovery and demographic change are visible. Rising income and education levels, and falling poverty, align with ACS data and gentrification trends.
Continued strong growth in income and education. Unemployment spike reflects COVID-19 pandemic impacts. Rising Gini coefficient suggests increasing inequality within the neighborhood.
Plausible annual estimates showing post-pandemic recovery and continuation of gentrification trends.
Plausible annual estimates showing post-pandemic recovery and continuation of gentrification trends.
